page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor architecture running at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration features 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a 30 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a top-tier rating in the Geekbench multi-core test and software compilation with Clang. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 6191.89 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 121.15 GB/sec. Single-core performance, however, is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category.

From a resource-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture provides dedicated hardware resources without virtualization overhead. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers high compute density, making it efficient for parallel processing tasks, though the lower memory-per-core ratio may limit memory-intensive applications. The system demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, achieving 102.32 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, and high throughput in static web serving. However, a notable tradeoff is observed in LLM text generation for small models, which registered a poor rating, though medium model text generation was strong. The server is suited for multi-threaded computing, software compilation, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 architecture running at 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in software compilation and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and static web serving capabilities. However, single-core performance remains average, and LLM text generation for small models is relatively poor. This server is suited for multi-threaded computing, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 ARMv8 Graviton cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.

tagline: A bare-metal ARMv8 platform with dedicated cores, DDR5 memory, and local NVMe storage optimized for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Graviton physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
128 GB of DDR5 memory paired with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core performance in software compilation and memory bandwidth
Strong database operations and static web serving throughput
Suited for multi-threaded computing, database hosting, and compilation tasks
